name: understanding-db-schema
description: Deep expertise in Logseq's Datascript database schema. Auto-invokes when users ask about Logseq DB schema, Datascript attributes, built-in classes, property types, entity relationships, schema validation, or the node/block/page data model. Provides authoritative knowledge of the DB graph architecture.
capabilities: Explains Datascript attribute types and cardinality options
Documents built-in class hierarchy with inheritance patterns
Describes property type system and validators
Provides templates for creating custom properties and classes
Details Malli validation flow and schema versioning
Covers node model with unified page and block concepts
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill providing read-only knowledge of Logseq's Datascript schema. Contains template examples and reference materials only. No executable code, no command execution, no file modifications. All 88 static findings are false positives from the scanner misidentifying Clojure Datalog keywords as cryptographic patterns and Clojure syntax backticks as shell execution.
valueStatement: Users need expert guidance to understand Logseq's Datascript database architecture for effective knowledge graph management. This skill provides authoritative knowledge of schema design, property types, class hierarchies, and entity relationships.
limitations: Does not execute Datalog queries on user data
Does not modify or write to user graphs
Cannot create or update actual schema definitions
Provides guidance only, not direct database access

useCases.title: Design custom properties
useCases.description: Create typed properties with validation, closed values, and cardinality for structured data.

useCases.title: Build Logseq plugins
useCases.description: Understand schema architecture to develop plugins that integrate with Logseq's database model.

useCases.title: Model relationships
useCases.description: Design class hierarchies and entity relationships for sophisticated knowledge management.

promptTemplates.title: Schema basics
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain the basic structure of Logseq's Datascript database including attribute types and cardinality options.

promptTemplates.title: Property creation
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I create a custom property with closed values and cardinality settings?

promptTemplates.title: Class inheritance
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a custom class that extends another class with associated properties.

promptTemplates.title: Query patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the common patterns for querying entities by class or property values?

bestPractices: Start with Root class and extend through inheritance for proper class hierarchy.
Use appropriate property types based on data requirements (default, number, date, checkbox).
Define closed values for properties with limited valid choices to ensure data consistency.
antiPatterns: Creating properties without specifying cardinality when multiple values are needed.
Extending wrong parent class causing unexpected behavior in queries.

faq.question: What versions of Logseq are supported?
faq.answer: This skill covers Logseq DB version 65.x and later with schema versioning support.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my Logseq database?
faq.answer: No. The skill only provides guidance. It cannot read or write to your actual graph files.

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: Read, Grep, Glob, WebFetch, and WebSearch for accessing reference materials.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is read-only and contains no code that accesses or modifies external data.

faq.question: How does this compare to other database skills?
faq.answer: This skill is Logseq-specific with deep knowledge of its unique Datascript schema and class system.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: Core knowledge is contained in the skill. WebFetch and WebSearch need internet for external docs.
